note: ChemReasoner (ICML 2024, arXiv:2402.10980v5). LLM heuristic tree search over catalyst space, pruned by BEAM search (width 6, depth 5, Nactions 8), reward = GNN computational surrogate (GemNet-dT trained on DFT) predicting adsorption energy; DFT (Quantum ESPRESSO) only post-hoc validation of top candidates. No physical/wet-lab execution. Both manuscript claims verified against the cited paper's full text.

C077.manuscript_claim: chemistry~\citep{...,sprueill2024chemreasoner,...} (ChemReasoner listed as an example under the 'chemistry' domain)

evidence.quote: ChemReasoner: Heuristic Search over a Large Language Model's Knowledge Space using Quantum-Chemical Feedback

The discovery of new catalysts is essential for the design of new and more efficient chemical processes... we introduce an AI-guided computational screening framework unifying linguistic reasoning with quantum-chemistry based feedback

C077.reasoning: ChemReasoner is squarely a chemistry/catalyst-discovery paper (heterogeneous catalysis, adsorption energies, CO2-to-methanol). Listing it under the 'chemistry' domain is correct.

C078.manuscript_claim: ChemCrow and ChemReasoner~\citep{sprueill2024chemreasoner} validate computationally with surrogate signals and search by beam.
C078.scope: Verifies the ChemReasoner half only; the ChemCrow half is a separate citation outside this paper (see reverse_findings for a coupling caveat).

evidence.quote: Each layer of the search tree is pruned using a beam search algorithm a beam search algorithm (Rubin & Reddy, 1977), leaving only those nodes with the highest rewards.
We use a beam width parameter (set to 6) to control the number of nodes that are expanded in each iteration of the search.
evidence.location: Section 5.2, p.8 (txt L628-630); max depth set to 5, Nactions set to 8

evidence.quote: A GNN (Gasteiger et al., 2021) is then used to calculate the adsorption energy from this state.
evidence.location: Section 3.3, p.6 (txt L448-450); reward model is GemNet-dT surrogate

evidence.claim_part: surrogate signals (surrogate stands in for DFT)
evidence.quote: the use of DFT-based rewards would be the gold standard reward calculations. However, due to their significant computational cost, we are forced to employ GNNs for high-throughput evaluation of catalyst candidates

C078.reasoning: Both predicates hold for ChemReasoner: (a) the search tree is explicitly pruned by beam search (width 6); (b) validation/scoring is by a GNN computational surrogate (GemNet-dT trained on DFT) predicting adsorption energy, with true DFT used only offline. Purely computational, no physical execution — consistent with 'validate computationally with surrogate signals'.

reverse_findings.fact: ChemReasoner's own search mechanism is BEAM search, not MCTS. MCTS (Monte Carlo Thought Search) belongs to the predecessor Sprueill et al. (2023), which this paper 'builds upon' but replaces LLM-computed rewards with computational-chemistry rewards and adds an LLM planner.
reverse_findings.loc: Title; Section 3.1 p.4; Section 2 p.3 ('we note that our method builds upon Sprueill et al. (2023)')
reverse_findings.mismatch: No mismatch for C078 (correctly says 'beam'). Guard: do NOT attribute MCTS to sprueill2024chemreasoner anywhere in the manuscript — MCTS is the earlier arXiv:2310.14420 paper.

reverse_findings.fact: The GNN surrogate diverges significantly from ground-truth DFT in several cases (e.g. CuZn *CHOH: GNN 0.552 eV vs DFT 5.951 eV; CuAlZn *OHCH3: GNN -1.589 vs DFT -5.615).
reverse_findings.mismatch: No mismatch — C078 only asserts 'surrogate signals', which is accurate. But the surrogate is explicitly NOT DFT-accurate; any manuscript wording implying DFT-grade or experimentally-validated accuracy would over-claim.

reverse_findings.fact: Validation is entirely computational (GNN in-loop + post-hoc DFT + literature comparison); there is no wet-lab synthesis or physical/robotic execution. Impact statement: goal is 'not necessarily to claim the discovery of a novel catalyst.'
reverse_findings.loc: Impact Statement p.9; Section 5.4 CO2 Hydrogenation case study p.9
reverse_findings.mismatch: No mismatch — C078 says 'validate computationally', which is exactly right. Flag only if any other manuscript claim implies experimental validation of ChemReasoner.

reverse_findings.fact: C078 couples ChemCrow and ChemReasoner as jointly 'search by beam' and 'validate computationally with surrogate signals'. Within ChemReasoner's own related-work, ChemCrow (Bran et al. 2023) is characterized as a tool-interfacing agent-based approach with LLM-computed guidance, not a beam-search / surrogate-reward system.
reverse_findings.loc: ChemReasoner Section 2.2 p.3 (cites Bran et al. 2023 as GPT-4 agent interfacing with chemistry tools; contrasts with its own domain-specific simulation-backed reward)
reverse_findings.mismatch: Possible over-generalization: 'search by beam' + 'surrogate signals' is verified true for ChemReasoner but may not hold for ChemCrow (a ReAct-style tool-augmented LLM). Recommend a separate ChemCrow-side check before treating the coupled sentence as fully accurate.
